name: He Meng
page_title: He Meng  [Lu Bu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	He Meng did not duel with Zhang Fei.
	Historically, He Meng rebelled against Lü Bu. This is not mentioned in the novel.
	He Meng was not executed by Cao Cao. He was beheaded by Cao Xing after rebelling against Lü Bu.

sections.novel:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	He Meng did not duel with Zhang Fei.
	Historically, He Meng rebelled against Lü Bu. This is not mentioned in the novel.
	He Meng was not executed by Cao Cao. He was beheaded by Cao Xing after rebelling against Lü Bu.
sections.short-biography: Officer of Lü Bu’s. Rebelled, but was defeated by Gao Shun.
sections.biography-source-undefined: He Meng was an officer under Lü Bu, who actually did very little to help his liege. In the novel, He Meng helps in Gao Shun’s battle against Xiahou Dun and Liu Bei. He also, in the novel, helps escort Wang Kai and Xu Si (two envoys of Lü Bu), to the land of Yuan Shu. Historically, neither of these things ever happened, because He Meng rebelled against Lü Bu in Xia Pi before Lü Bu was besieged by Cao Cao. He planned this revolt at nighttime, and forced Lü Bu to flee in his night robes to the camp of Gao Shun. Fortunately for Lü Bu, Gao Shun was able to defeat He Meng, and Cao Xing, another officer under Lü Bu, later beheaded the rebel.

full_text:
He Meng
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
He Mengwas born in Henei. Officer of Lü Bu’s. Rebelled, but was defeated by Gao Shun. He Meng was affiliated with Lu Bu.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
He Meng did not duel with Zhang Fei.
Historically, He Meng rebelled against Lü Bu. This is not mentioned in the novel.
He Meng was not executed by Cao Cao. He was beheaded by Cao Xing after rebelling against Lü Bu.
Literary Appearances
Romance of the Three Kingdoms:18,19
BiographySource Undefined
He Meng was an officer under Lü Bu, who actually did very little to help his liege. In the novel, He Meng helps in Gao Shun’s battle against Xiahou Dun and Liu Bei. He also, in the novel, helps escort Wang Kai and Xu Si (two envoys of Lü Bu), to the land of Yuan Shu. Historically, neither of these things ever happened, because He Meng rebelled against Lü Bu in Xia Pi before Lü Bu was besieged by Cao Cao. He planned this revolt at nighttime, and forced Lü Bu to flee in his night robes to the camp of Gao Shun. Fortunately for Lü Bu, Gao Shun was able to defeat He Meng, and Cao Xing, another officer under Lü Bu, later beheaded the rebel.
